The Selection
i.

About the artist

David Feighery is a Dublin-based photographer drawn to the city's coastal edges — its piers, chimneys, swimming clubs, and the ever-shifting Irish skies that sit above them. His work moves between long-exposure stillness and dramatic, weather-charged moments.

Working across both colour and black and white, David approaches the familiar landmarks of Dublin Bay and the surrounding coast with patience, returning to the same locations across seasons and tides to find them in new light.

His photography is collected at davidfeighery.ie, where prints and the wider archive are available.

Privacy & cookies

On this page
  1. Overview
  2. What we collect
  3. Why we use it
  4. Cookies
  5. Your rights (GDPR)
  6. Retention
  7. Contact & complaints

Last updated: April 2026

Overview

This site is the personal photography portfolio of David Feighery, based in Dublin, Ireland. We take your privacy seriously and aim to collect as little personal data as possible. This page explains, in plain language, what we collect, why, how long we keep it, and the rights you have under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Irish Data Protection Act 2018.

For the purposes of GDPR, the data controller is David Feighery, contactable at info@davidfeighery.ie.

What we collect

We only collect personal information that you choose to give us. Specifically:

  • Contact form submissions. If you fill out the contact form, we receive your name, email address, subject line, and message content.
  • Email correspondence. If you email us directly, we receive whatever information you include in that email.
  • Basic technical data. Like most websites, our hosting provider may automatically log standard request information (IP address, browser type, page requested, timestamp) for security and reliability purposes.

We do not collect payment information, location data, or sensitive personal data through this website.

Why we use your data

We use the information above only for the following purposes:

  • To respond to enquiries you send us through the contact form or by email (legal basis: legitimate interest, and/or pre-contractual steps at your request).
  • To keep the website secure and operational (legal basis: legitimate interest).

We do not use your data for marketing, profiling, or automated decision-making. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal information with third parties for their own marketing purposes.

Cookies

This website uses a minimal approach to cookies and similar technologies:

  • Strictly necessary cookies / local storage. We store a small flag in your browser to remember whether you have dismissed the cookie notice, so we don't show it on every visit. This requires no consent under the ePrivacy Directive as it is strictly necessary for a feature you have requested.
  • Analytics cookies. This site does not currently use Google Analytics or any third-party analytics tracking. If this changes in the future, we will request your consent before any non-essential cookies are set.
  • Third-party content. Pages do not embed third-party widgets (social plugins, video players, maps) that would set cookies on your behalf.

You can clear cookies and local storage at any time through your browser settings. Doing so will reset the cookie notice.

Your rights under GDPR

If you are in the EU/EEA, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

  • Right of access — to request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
  • Right to rectification — to ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete information.
  • Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten") — to ask us to delete your personal data.
  • Right to restrict processing — to ask us to limit how we use your data.
  • Right to data portability — to receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
  • Right to object — to object to processing based on legitimate interests.
  • Right to withdraw consent — where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw it at any time.

To exercise any of these rights, email us at info@davidfeighery.ie. We will respond within one calendar month, as required by GDPR.

How long we keep your data

Contact form submissions and email correspondence are kept only as long as needed to respond to your enquiry and any reasonable follow-up — typically no longer than 24 months — after which they are deleted. Server logs are retained by our hosting provider for a short period (typically 30 days) for security purposes.

Contact & complaints

If you have any questions about how your data is handled, please contact us first at info@davidfeighery.ie — we are happy to help.

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Irish Data Protection Commission, the supervisory authority for data protection in Ireland: www.dataprotection.ie.

A quick note on cookies. This site uses only one strictly necessary item in your browser's local storage — to remember that you've seen this notice. No analytics, no tracking, no third parties. Read the full policy.
